title: C1 — Init-time probe README + docs to fixate knowledge_language (reuse scan.ts detectExistingLanguage)
description: In writeDefaultFabricConfig, only when .fabric/fabric-config.json is absent, invoke scan.ts L586-L644 detectExistingLanguage(target) which scans README.md + docs/*.md for CJK ratio. Fixate knowledge_language to 'zh-CN' (ratio>0.3) or 'en' (default). Never overwrite existing user config. Hoist detectExistingLanguage to a shared module if cross-package import is awkward, OR call via direct path import.

files.change: Add detectExistingLanguage(target) call BEFORE the FABRIC_CONFIG_DEFAULTS object construction; replace knowledge_language='match-existing' literal (L400) with the resolved value; preserve idempotency early-return at L394
If not already exported, export the function; verify it accepts a project-root path argument (refactor if currently bound to scan internals)
Add 3 scenarios: (a) README with >30% CJK → init writes knowledge_language='zh-CN', (b) pure-English README → 'en', (c) empty repo → 'en' (default)

implementation: Read scan.ts L586-L644 detectExistingLanguage; verify signature accepts a project-root absolute path
If function is module-private, export it (add to scan.ts exports) — alternatively extract to a shared helper in packages/cli/src/lib/ if init.ts shouldn't import from scan.ts

In init.ts writeDefaultFabricConfig, after the existence check at L394 but before defaults serialization, call `const detectedLanguage = detectExistingLanguage(targetRoot)` and assign to knowledge_language
Preserve the L394 early-return — fixation only happens on fresh init, never overwrites existing user config (per init.ts:386-390 NEVER-overwrite policy)
Add scan-init.test.ts cases using mkdtemp fixtures: zh-CN repo with CJK README, en repo with English README, empty repo

reference.pattern: Reuse scan.ts CJK ratio probe; respect init.ts idempotency contract
reference.examples: detectExistingLanguage reads README.md + docs/*.md (one level deep), counts CJK Unified Ideographs vs ASCII letters, ratio>0.3 → zh-CN else en; empty repo → en

criteria: Fresh init in repo with CJK README writes knowledge_language='zh-CN' to .fabric/fabric-config.json
Fresh init in English-only repo writes knowledge_language='en'
Fresh init in empty repo writes knowledge_language='en' (default)
scan-init.test.ts has 3+ new scenarios; all green
detectExistingLanguage is reachable from init.ts without circular import

rationale.chosen_approach: Reuse scan.ts detectExistingLanguage as-is (README+docs CJK probe); init-time fixation only on fresh init
alternatives_considered: Probe package.json#description (skews English for npm-named projects)
Weighted multi-source (README + docs + package.json) with bias
Always write 'match-existing' and resolve at scan time (preserves legacy behavior)
decision_factors: Proven detector with existing test coverage
package.json is almost always English ASCII → skews detection
Idempotent init means burn-in is safe (never overwrites)
rationale.tradeoffs: Repos that gain a CJK README post-init keep their 'en' default; users can manually edit fabric-config.json — accepted

manual_checks: mkdir tmpdir + write CJK README + run `fab init` → cat .fabric/fabric-config.json shows zh-CN
success_metrics: Idempotency preserved (no overwrite on second init)
Detector function exported cleanly without circular import
